title: VERIFY-2 impl/QA report artifact (verify/report.M<id>.json)
description: The proof-of-work the VERIFY-2 Verify+Fix (impl/QA) node writes PER MILESTONE. Written by packages/skills/verify/SKILL.md after booting the built game headless, proving the build is a FAITHFUL realization of VERIFY-1's frozen blueprint: build-health, USER-FLOW FIDELITY (the blueprint mechanisms as Given/When/Then), COMPLETABILITY (replay the reference intended solution), trace-level INVARIANTS, and ISOMORPHIC PERTURBATION (re-run the acceptance criteria with the blueprint's declared-range parameters permuted), then running the bounded <=3 self-fix on failure and emitting the VALIDATION_PASSED/FAILED marker. Read by the orchestrator (the binary marker is the gate) and by a human (per-check observed-vs-expected, the perturbation divergences, screenshots, fix trail). INTENTIONAL CHANGE vs the original W5 shape: written PER MILESTONE (verify/report.M2.json), NEVER overwritten, so the full run's evidence persists (the old node wrote/overwrote a single verify/report.json — SKILL §9). ADDITIVE schema extension: the original assertions[]/buildHealth/fixCycles/advisoryVlm/regression/screenshots fields are retained (an old verify/report.json still validates); the VERIFY-2 gate fields (fidelity/completability/invariants/perturbation/escalation) are OPTIONAL additions. Evidence: research/skills/w5-verify-research.md synthesis #10; SKILL §6 + §9 + perturbation-grammar.md.

milestoneId.description: The milestone this report verifies (e.g. 'M2'). 1 milestone <-> 1 report <-> 1 marker.

marker.description: The verbatim marker emitted to stdout and parsed by the orchestrator. PASSED iff every assertion passed AND the game booted/became ready with no fatal console error. A crash / never-ready / missing marker is FAILED. This is the binary gate; nothing else blocks it.

passed.description: marker === 'VALIDATION_PASSED'. Redundant convenience field for non-string consumers.

summary.description: One-line human summary, also the marker's trailing message. PASSED: '<id> all N assertions passed'. FAILED: the joined failed-assertion describes (the gamedevbench '; '-joined issues).

assertions.description: Per-assertion result, 1:1 with the milestone's gdd assertions (which are 1:1 with acceptanceCriteria). EVERY assertion runs (single-aggregate, not fail-fast), so a human sees all failures at once. These mechanic assertions are AUTHORITATIVE: any 'fail' here forces marker=FAILED.

describe.description: The gdd assertion's human-readable predicate = its failure message (e.g. 'pressing Up decreases player.y within 300ms').

comparator.description: The single gdd 'expect' comparator key that was set.

expected.description: The comparator's expected value/direction. For relative comparators (decreases/increases/changes/unchanged) this is the boolean direction; for equals/atLeast/atMost it is the target number|string|boolean.

observed.description: What W5 actually read off __GAME__ after firing the input. For relative comparators, an object {before, after}. null if the observe path was missing/undefined on the hook.

status.description: 'pass' = comparator satisfied; 'fail' = comparator not satisfied on a clean read; 'error' = the observe path was missing on __GAME__, the input couldn't be fired, or a read threw (treated as fail for the marker, recorded distinctly for diagnosis).

message.description: On fail/error: the diagnostic (e.g. 'expected player.y to decrease; before=300 after=300 (unchanged)' or 'observe path player.dashCount not present on __GAME__'). Omitted on pass.

screenshot.description: Optional path to a screenshot captured at this assertion's check point (relative to the project dir).

buildHealth.description: Build-health context. W4 guarantees a green build on entry (it is W4's pre-gate, not W5's). W5 only re-runs the build if a self-fix EDITS src/** (a fix that breaks the build is a failed fix cycle).

greenOnEntry.description: Was 'npm run build' green when W5 received the milestone (W4's guarantee). If false, W5 records it and proceeds to runtime verify only if the prior build artifact boots; a non-building project should not reach W5.

rebuiltAfterFix.description: Did W5 re-run 'npm run build' because a self-fix edited src/**?

greenAfterFix.description: Result of the post-fix build re-check (omit if no fix edited src/**). A red post-fix build is a failed fix cycle.

fixCycles.description: Number of bounded self-fix cycles run (0 = passed first try, the happy path an extractor records). Capped at 3.

fixEdits.description: The src/** edits each fix cycle made, for audit + anti-reward-hack review. Empty when fixCycles=0.

files.description: src/** files edited this cycle. MUST be under src/** game code only; NEVER spec/gdd.json, the assertions, or the __GAME__ hook adapter (anti-reward-hack: the oracle is outside the fix edit set).

rationale.description: The real-behavior root cause fixed (e.g. 'ArrowUp was not wired to PlatformerMovement.jump() -> wired it'; 'score was a scene field, not registry.set -> wrote to registry').

fixOutcome.description: How the verify resolved. passed_first_try (fixCycles=0); fixed (a cycle made all assertions pass); exhausted (3 cycles, still failing); stalled (same failure signature repeated -> stopped early); boot_failed (never became ready / fatal console error -> did not burn fix cycles).

advisoryVlm.description: The ADVISORY screenshot review. NEVER blocks the marker in v1 (VLM precision ~0.50; deterministic mechanic assertions are the authority). Records a triage signal only.

ran.description: Was a VLM review run? May be false (v1 leaves it optional).

flag.description: Coarse 3-value advisory flag (VLMs can rank but not score). 'looks_off' is a TRIAGE hint for a human, never a marker gate.

canvasNotBlank.description: The cheap deterministic 'canvas has content' check (toDataURL().length > 1000). A blank canvas with passing assertions is a notable advisory signal (the hook may be true while nothing renders).

note.description: The VLM's one-line rationale, if any.

regression.description: Pass-to-pass guard: because milestones build cumulatively, after a self-fix VERIFY-2 re-checks that prior milestones' fidelity checks still pass (SWE-bench PASS_TO_PASS; MobileGym unexpected-side-effects). Omitted if no fix edited src/**. SKILL §9 regression guard.

priorMilestonesChecked.description: Prior milestone ids whose fidelity checks were re-run after the fix.

broke.description: Prior check ids that NOW fail after this milestone's fix (a regression the fix introduced). Empty = no regression. A non-empty list is a real problem recorded for the orchestrator/human.

fidelity.description: VERIFY-2 §3 USER-FLOW FIDELITY: each blueprint mechanism as Given/When/Then, driven from a KNOWN precondition VERIFY-2 placed. 1:1 with blueprint.acceptanceCriteria (the canonical fidelity contract; the gdd assertions[] are the executable fallback). EVERY mechanism runs (single-aggregate, not fail-fast). Any 'fail'/'error' here forces marker=FAILED. OPTIONAL (the original assertions[] block remains valid); a VERIFY-2 report carries fidelity[] instead of/alongside assertions[].

id.description: The acceptance-criterion / gdd assertion id (e.g. 'M2-A1' or 'AC-M3-win').

describe.description: The mechanism's human-readable predicate = its failure message.

given.description: The precondition VERIFY-2 placed (the frozen GIVEN from blueprint.acceptanceCriteria). MUST be a legal precondition per perturbation-grammar.md §6 (D2): not the observed field, not a one-step causal input the mechanism reads to produce the outcome.

comparator.description: The single 'expect' comparator key that was set.

expected.description: The comparator's expected value/direction (boolean direction for relative comparators; number|string|boolean for equals/atLeast/atMost).

observed.description: What VERIFY-2 read off __GAME__ after firing the input. For relative comparators, an object {before, after}. null if the observe path was missing/undefined.

status.description: 'pass' = comparator satisfied; 'fail' = not satisfied on a clean read; 'error' = observe path missing / input couldn't fire / read threw (treated as fail for the marker, recorded distinctly).

message.description: On fail/error: the diagnostic. Omitted on pass.

screenshot.description: Optional path to a screenshot captured at this check's end state (relative to the project dir).

completability.description: VERIFY-2 §4 COMPLETABILITY: replay blueprint.referenceSolution (the proven winning action-sequence) step-by-step and assert it reaches the win THROUGH REAL PLAY (interim observables hold, never via setState injection). Proves the build realizes the proven-winnable design. OPTIONAL (present on a VERIFY-2 report).

reachedWin.description: Did the replay reach the win observable (status==='won' + the archetype's completion invariants) through the real action-sequence?

interimObservables.description: Per-step interim observable checks along the replay (e.g. 'score reached 3 through real collection'), proving the win came via the genuine flow, not an injected shortcut.

status.description: 'pass' = win reached through real play; 'fail' = a step mis-implements (door doesn't latch, coin doesn't increment, RESPAWN wrong) -> IMPLEMENTATION FAILURE (fixable §7); 'error' = replay could not run (verdict-correctness §11). A blueprint solution that no longer holds even when built faithfully is a DESIGN ESCALATION (§8), recorded in escalation, not a fail here.

message.description: On fail/error: the diagnostic (which step, observed-vs-expected).

invariants.description: VERIFY-2 §5 trace-level + differential pre/post-conditions, sampled on a bounded cadence across every drive (§3) and the replay (§4) — relations an end-state check misses (score-monotone, in-bounds, no soft-lock, status-legality, no unexpected side-effect). A violation is a real bug -> FAILED. OPTIONAL (present on a VERIFY-2 report).

name.description: Human name of the invariant (e.g. 'score never decreases within a life').

held.description: Did the invariant hold across the whole trace? false = a real bug forcing FAILED.

evidence.description: On a violation: the trace evidence (e.g. 'player frozen at x=308 for ~900 frames under continuous input' or 'status flipped to lost on contact in a respawn-only design').

perturbation.description: VERIFY-2 §6 ISOMORPHIC PERTURBATION (the load-bearing anti-gaming gate): a second bounded boot that re-runs the acceptance criteria + completability with blueprint.declaredRanges parameters PERMUTED within their declared bands. A faithful build is INVARIANT; a contorted/hard-coded/over-fit build DIVERGES. invariant===false forces marker=FAILED. Applied via commands.{reset,seed,setState} / config-injection ONLY, NEVER by editing src/** or any oracle, NEVER widening a declared range. Contract: perturbation-grammar.md. OPTIONAL on the schema, but NON-OPTIONAL on a VERIFY-2 run (a verify without the permutation pass is INCOMPLETE, SKILL §11).

ran.description: Was the permuted re-run executed? false only on a missing/empty blueprint.declaredRanges (a VERIFY-1 contract gap -> design escalation, NOT a build pass).

permutationSeed.description: The deterministic selector seed (seed/index-based, reproducible — NEVER raw Math.random()). A re-verify reproduces the exact permuted run. perturbation-grammar.md §2.

permutationsApplied.description: One entry per parameterPath actually moved (point-ranges that couldn't move are omitted).

parameterPath.description: The flat declaredRanges key permuted (e.g. 'config.enemyWalkSpeed', 'layout.rewards.coin_1.x', 'rng.seed'). The engine enumerates declaredRanges as parameterPath -> [min,max].

original.description: The frozen blueprint value for this parameterPath.

permuted.description: The in-envelope permuted value drawn from [min,max] (strictly inside the declared range; never widened).

seam.description: Where the permutation was applied (D1): 'runtime' = commands.setState/seed/injected level-config (positions, seed, counts, precondition placement, patrol phase); 'baked-config' = permuted gameConfig.json + EXACTLY ONE rebuild (a tunable read once in create(), e.g. enemyWalkSpeed).

invariant.description: true = every originally-passing acceptance criterion + the completability replay still pass under the permutation (faithful build). false = at least one passed originally but FAILS in-envelope (contorted build) -> forces marker=FAILED. A permutation that broke the design is a HARNESS bug (§11 self-guard), corrected and re-run, NOT recorded as a divergence.

diverged.description: Empty when invariant===true. Each entry localizes a contortion for the fix step.

checkId.description: The acceptance criterion / completability id that diverged (passed originally, failed under permutation).

permutation.description: The permutation class + the specific move that exposed the contortion (e.g. 'approach-distance (precondition re-placed +18px within [380,460])', 'count 3->5', 'coord-shift', 'seed 1->777', 'enemyWalkSpeed 80->90').

originalObserved.description: What __GAME__ read under the ORIGINAL parameters (the passing value).

permutedObserved.description: What __GAME__ read under the PERMUTED parameters (the failing value) — the evidence the build over-fit the exact numbers.

escalation.description: VERIFY-2 §8 DESIGN ESCALATION: present IFF a genuine DESIGN problem (the frozen blueprint itself is wrong — a step unwinnable even when built faithfully, a contradiction VERIFY-1 missed) was flagged and routed UPSTREAM (to VERIFY-1 / the design node), NEVER 'fixed' by bending the build. Mirrors blueprint/escalations.M<id>.json. Present only on a design escalation, never a substitute for fixing a real implementation bug.

milestoneId.description: The milestone whose blueprint is being escalated.

kind.description: The escalation class. VERIFY-2 only escalates design defects (implementation bugs are fixed in §7, not escalated).

evidence.description: The evidence the blueprint is at fault: which check, what was observed, what the blueprint expected.

note.description: One-line statement of the design defect routed upstream.

screenshots.description: Paths (relative to the project dir) of screenshots captured: at least one at the milestone's end state, one per failure, and the permuted-run end state. Used by a human and by the advisory VLM.

consoleErrors.description: Console errors + uncaught pageerrors captured during boot/run. A fatal error during boot is a boot_failed fail-fast. Fed to the fix step.

durationMs.description: Wall-clock duration of the whole milestone verify (boot + assertions + any fix cycles).

startedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this milestone verify started.
